purpose: Machine-readable definition-of-done controls for WellFit task types. Future agents must select the matching key before implementation and report it in the PR output.
sharedCriteria: AGENTS.md and scoped governance files were read before changes.
Work happened on a task-specific branch, not directly on main.
Existing leading files from todolist/WORK_MAP.md and project-register were extended instead of creating duplicate architecture.
Protected Unity/PR #13 areas were not touched unless explicitly approved, and no such approval is implied by default.

Product logic, compliance-critical logic, reward authority, token/NFT/wallet/payment/betting, health, child, location, privacy, and legal logic were not modified unless the selected task type explicitly allowed it with human approval.
Required checks were run or documented with exact failure reasons and environment limitations.
PR summary includes changed files, checks, risks, dependency status, and next recommended task.

doneWhen: Only Markdown or approved planning documents changed.
Documentation points to the leading source-of-truth files instead of replacing them.
Historical TODO/status context was preserved; stale or duplicate content was marked and linked rather than deleted.
No runtime code, product behavior, legal copy, privacy copy, or compliance-sensitive wording changed.
Relevant cross-references in WORK_MAP.md or TODO_INDEX.md were updated when a new documentation file was added.
requiredEvidence: Statement that runtime code and protected areas were untouched.

doneWhen: JSON is syntactically valid and follows existing project-register conventions.
New entries reference existing leading files, routes, APIs, features, or docs and do not invent unimplemented behavior as fact.
Machine-readable entries include status, purpose, source links, and safety boundaries where relevant.
WORK_MAP.md and TODO_INDEX.md point to newly added governance registers when required.
No runtime code or product logic changed.
requiredEvidence: jq validation commands for changed JSON files.
Source files used to justify registry entries.

doneWhen: Only existing route/page/component surfaces needed for the scoped UI fix changed.
The current visual identity, AppShell, sidebar, footer, mobile navigation, legal/help flow, and mission route structure remain intact unless explicitly requested.
Route/page/feature registers are updated if route behavior or inventory changed.
No API authority, reward logic, health/location/camera/child/privacy logic, wallet/payment/token/NFT/betting behavior, or legal/compliance copy changed.
A screenshot or preview note is provided for perceptible runnable UI changes when the environment supports it.
requiredEvidence: Screenshot/preview note for perceptible UI changes.
Lint, typecheck, build, and quality-gate results.

doneWhen: API authority level is documented in project-register/apis.json before or with the change.
Security boundaries, allowed inputs/outputs, no-secret posture, and data minimization are explicit.
Economy/reward APIs remain preview/draft-only unless an explicit human-approved server-authority plan is present.
No final ledger, payment, wallet, token/NFT, betting, health, child, location, privacy, or compliance-sensitive behavior is introduced without explicit approval.
Tests/checks cover syntax and build impact, and any missing emulator requirements are documented.
requiredEvidence: Check results including build and relevant function checks.

doneWhen: Existing mission routes, libs, and registers from WORK_MAP.md are extended rather than replaced.
Mission completion, reward authorization, anti-cheat, evidence, and rare-item grants are not finalized on the client.
Any points/XP behavior is clearly internal beta preview/draft unless server authority is explicitly approved.
Affected routes/APIs/features and mission architecture docs are updated when behavior changes.
No PvP stake, betting, payout, wallet, token/NFT, or marketplace authority is activated.
Existing Buddy/KI pages, components, libs, API route, and architecture docs are extended in place.
Buddy/KI can guide, explain, personalize, or summarize but cannot authorize rewards, points, mission completion, tokens, payments, or ledger writes.
Provider, prompt, model, and fallback behavior are documented without exposing secrets to the frontend.
Child-safety, health, location, privacy, and sensitive-profile assumptions are not expanded without explicit approval.
Feature/API registers remain aligned with the Buddy/KI boundaries.
Backend, Functions, Firestore, or rules changes are minimal and tied to existing architecture docs/registers.
No client or untrusted caller receives final reward, ledger, payment, wallet, token/NFT, betting, or mission-completion authority.
Rules/callables avoid secret exposure and document auth, validation, rate/cooldown, and data-minimization assumptions.
Emulator or syntax-check requirements are run where available; environment blockers are clearly classified.
Relevant backend readiness, security, API, and feature registers are synchronized.
Feedback/analytics work follows project-register/user-feedback.json and USER_FEEDBACK_DATABASE_FLOW.md.
No third-party analytics, live tracking, raw user-data agent reports, or sensitive data collection is added without explicit approval.
Consent, retention, minimization, allowed fields, forbidden fields, and aggregate-summary boundaries are documented.
Feedback APIs or implementation work stays server-validated and avoids direct client Firestore writes unless approved by existing architecture.
Registers and docs remain synchronized.
Work is planning/inventory-only unless the user explicitly approves runtime AR work.
native/unity/WellFitBuddyAR and PR #13 are untouched and not used as shortcuts.
Unity/AR is described as event/rendering input; app/backend retain reward, mission-completion, and anti-cheat authority.
Existing AR architecture docs, TODO entries, and internal-source mappings are linked instead of creating a parallel AR plan.
No native files, AR runtime code, reward logic, health/location/camera/privacy behavior, or Unity scripts changed.
Work remains planning-only and does not modify runtime behavior or user-facing legal/compliance copy.
Protected area, risk level, assumptions, unknowns, and required human/legal/security decisions are explicit.

No token/NFT/wallet/payment/purchase/payout/marketplace/staking/presale/trading, betting/PvP stake, health/watch/location/camera/child/privacy/consent, legal, or reward-authority logic changes are made.
Any future implementation is broken into approval-gated tasks with protected files listed.
Registers and architecture docs point back to AGENTS.md safety rules and risk-classifier stop rules.
requiredEvidence: Explicit human approval list for future work.
